task:
# Relay stage 1: Ideate
Task: followup-rename-history-and-newtask-title
Working directory: /tmp/nix-shell.0TxEfP/visual-relay/wt/b355188660a3/plan-20260624121456/followup-rename-history-and-newtask-title

## Task input
# Task rename orphans run history; new-task-with-body shows slug instead of title

Two adjacent correctness gaps surfaced by review of the `show-full-task-name` feature
(commit `42a45f8`, which added a detail-panel task name that is editable, with save
renaming the task's markdown file and subfolder). The feature itself works; these are
follow-ups.

## Problem 1 — renaming a task severs its run history

`RelayTaskWriter.RenameAsync` renames the task's `llm-tasks/<slug>/` subfolder and its
`<slug>.md`, but does **not** move the task's run-history directory `.relay/<slug>/`.
Run artifacts/metrics/status/seals/ledger are keyed by task id under
`.relay/<taskId>/` (see `RelayRunHistory`, which builds paths like
`Path.Combine(rootPath, ".relay", taskId)`). So renaming a task that has already run
severs its history — the detail pane then shows "No run history", and a stale
`.relay/<old-slug>/` directory is left behind.

**Fix:** when renaming, if `.relay/<old-slug>/` exists, move it to `.relay/<new-slug>/`
(in the same operation that renames the `llm-tasks` subfolder, with the same safety
checks — no overwrite of an existing destination). Also migrate the in-memory id-keyed
tracking state in `MainWindowViewModel` so a rename during the session doesn't orphan
live state: `SaveEditAsync` currently migrates only `_boostedTaskIds`. Factor a single
"re-key task id across all id-keyed maps" helper and call it for the maps that can hold
entries for a renamable (non-running) task — at minimum `_liveEventsByTask` and
`_liveTraceEntriesByTask` (others like `_runStartedAt` are for running tasks, which
`CanEditSelectedTask` already blocks from editing; include them for completeness).

## Problem 2 — a new task created *with a body* shows the slug, not the entered title

In `CreateNewTaskAsync` (`MainWindowViewModel.Authoring.cs`), the with-body branch writes
`markdown = NewTaskBody` verbatim — the entered `NewTaskTitle` is used only to derive the
slug and is then discarded. With no `# ` heading in the file, the new detail-panel name
(`ExtractTitleFromMarkdown`) falls back to the slug, so the panel shows e.g.
`my-new-task` instead of "My New Task". The empty-body branch already writes `# {title}\n`
correctly.

**Fix:** prepend the heading to the body branch too, matching `SaveEditAsync`'s format
(`# {title}\n\n{body}`): write `# {NewTaskTitle.Trim()}\n\n{NewTaskBody}`.

## Problem 3 (low, optional) — title-extraction asymmetry

`ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` scans all lines for the first `# ` heading, but
`SplitMarkdownTitle` inspects only the first line. For markdown whose heading isn't on
line 1 (e.g. a leading blank line), select shows the real title while Edit puts the slug
in the title buffer, so saving unchanged triggers a spurious rename and a duplicated
heading. Make both helpers use the same "first `# ` line" strategy.

## How to verify
- Rename a task **that has already run**: its run history (metrics/status/artifacts)
  still resolves after the rename, `.relay/<new-slug>/` exists, and no stale
  `.relay/<old-slug>/` remains. Add a test around `RenameAsync` asserting the `.relay`
  dir moves when present (and is a no-op when absent).
- Create a **new task with a body**: the detail panel "Markdown" tab shows the entered
  title (not the slug). Add/extend a test in the new-task authoring tests.
- Existing rename/title tests stay green.

## Constraints
- VR is general-purpose — no project-specific assumptions; derive everything generically.
- Keep C#/XAML files ≤300 lines (some touched files are already at the limit — relocate a
  helper if needed rather than exceeding it). `./visual-relay check` green; Conventional
  Commit subjects.


## Manifest
(not set yet)

## Prior stages


End your reply with a single fenced ```json block, nothing after it, matching: { "summary": string, "options": string[] }

result.answer:
Based on the detailed task description, here are the three problems and the solution options:

**Problem 1 — Rename orphans run history:** `RenameAsync` doesn't move `.relay/<old-slug>/` and `MainWindowViewModel.SaveEditAsync` only migrates `_boostedTaskIds`, leaving `_liveEventsByTask`, `_liveTraceEntriesByTask`, and other id-keyed maps stale.

**Problem 2 — New-task-with-body shows slug:** `CreateNewTaskAsync`'s with-body branch discards `NewTaskTitle` — no `# ` heading in the written markdown, so `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` falls back to the slug.

**Problem 3 — Title-extraction asymmetry:** `SplitMarkdownTitle` inspects only line 1 while `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` scans all lines for `# ` — so a heading not on line 1 causes a discrepancy between Select (shows real title) and Edit (shows slug).

```json
{
  "summary": "Fix two correctness gaps in the `show-full-task-name` feature (commit 42a45f8): (1) `RenameAsync` severs run history by not moving `.relay/<old-slug>/` and not re-keying in-memory maps in `MainWindowViewModel`; (2) `CreateNewTaskAsync`'s with-body branch writes `NewTaskBody` verbatim without a `# ` heading, so the detail panel falls back to the slug. Optionally (3) `SplitMarkdownTitle` and `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` use different heading-detection strategies, causing a Select/Edit inconsistency.",
  "options": [
    "Option A — Minimal targeted fixes in-place: (1) In `RenameAsync`, add `.relay/<old-slug>/` → `.relay/<new-slug>/` directory move with the same no-overwrite guard used for `llm-tasks/`. In `SaveEditAsync`, add manual re-key calls for `_liveEventsByTask` and `_liveTraceEntriesByTask` (and the others for completeness). (2) In `CreateNewTaskAsync`'s with-body branch, change the write to `$'# {NewTaskTitle.Trim()}\\n\\n{NewTaskBody}'`. (3) Change `SplitMarkdownTitle` to scan all lines for the first `# ` heading, matching `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown`. Low effort, highest blast-radius for the 300-line cap on already-near-limit files.",
    "Option B — Extract helpers for reuse: Same fixes as A, but (1) extract a single `RekeyTaskId(string oldId, string newId)` helper in `MainWindowViewModel` that migrates all id-keyed dictionaries, including `_boostedTaskIds`, `_liveEventsByTask`, `_liveTraceEntriesByTask`, `_runStartedAt`, etc. (2) Extract a `FirstHeadingLine(string markdown) → int?` helper (or `TryGetFirstHeading`) shared by both `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` and `SplitMarkdownTitle`, eliminating the asymmetry. (3) If `MainWindowViewModel.cs` approaches 300 lines after the re-key helper, relocate the heading helpers into a small `MarkdownHelpers.cs` static class. Keeps each file ≤300 lines, adds testable seam for the re-key logic.",
    "Option C — Hardened with extracted service and tests: Same as B, but (1) pull the directory-rename logic (including `.relay/` migration) out of `RelayTaskWriter.RenameAsync` into a standalone `TaskRenamerService` that coordinates `llm-tasks/` rename + `.relay/` move + id re-key in one operation; `RenameAsync` becomes a thin delegate. (2) Add explicit test coverage: a test asserting `.relay/<old-slug>/` is moved when present and silently skipped when absent, and a new-task-with-body test asserting the written markdown starts with `# <title>`. (3) The shared heading helper uses a single `static` method that returns the first `# ` heading line (or null), consumed by both `ExtractTitleFromMarkdown` and `SplitMarkdownTitle`. Highest structural change but best long-term maintainability and test isolation."
  ]
}
```

Let me formulate options based on those details.

Now I'll formulate the three options clearly. Let me organize them around the three problems.

Option A: Minimal targeted fixes — in-place changes per the task description, no refactoring.
Option B: Extract helpers for reuse — create a re-key helper and a shared heading-extraction helper, keeping files under 300 lines.
Option C: Hardened approach — same as B but also create test coverage and possibly extract a dedicated service.
